My research investigates how generative artificial intelligence reshapes normative behaviors, creative production, and labor market dynamics in the digital economy. I ground my research agenda in the legal landscape, using emerging copyright lawsuits involving authors and artists against AI firms as a lens to narrow my focus toward the most pressing challenges in data governance and AI policy.

I combine causal inference with Deep Learning, LLMs, and multimodal feature extraction to operationalize theory-driven and industry-defined constructs. These constructs allow me to quantify the impact of interventions on human creative behaviors and the broader creative economy.
